We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Person (GFP):

Submit GAD database, regularly update the same, and conduct gender analysis as basis for addressing gender issues and concerns, pursuant to regulations.

Prepare and submit the GAD ARs following the prescribed format as provided in Annex E of the PCW- DILG –DBM- NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Implemented | The GAD Focal Person was unable to submit the GAD HGDG Checklist/Tool due to lack of manpower. (See related finding under Part II of this report-Observation No. 8).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the required Schedule of Accounts Payable and the accompanying supporting documents of the accumulated accounts payable amounting to ₱2.73 million as reflected in the financial statements   as of December 31, 2018   to back up the authenticity of the recorded obligations in the books of accounts of the Municipal Government. We suggested that in-depth evaluation be undertaken to identify the veracity, nature and claim of the recorded payables both for the current and prior years ledgers/files to determine which are valid claims or fictitious accounts. Moreover, for those established transactions with no valid claimants and dormant accounts, the Office of the Municipal Accountant must take up the appropriate reversion/adjustments in the books of the LGU for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Lack of initiative to comply with the audit recommendations and due to limited manpower at the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer provide all the pertinent records on the Uson Public Market leaseholders to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the latter to set up the receivable accounts and record its corresponding subsidiary ledgers in the books of accounts of the Municipal Government. Close coordination of the aforementioned Offices is further recommended to evaluate the appropriate amount of receivables from the leaseholders to be recorded in the books of the LGU in order to reflect the accurate balances of receivables and income accounts.
observations.reason: Previously assigned Market Supervisor has resigned and no records has been turned over to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the mandated frequency or time frame governing deposit to AGDB and deposit intact all his collections as well as collections turned-over to him by the collectors daily or not later than the next banking day as prescribed.  Also, require all Accountable Officers to desist from handling huge amount of cash in their possession and deposit all their collections in accordance with the NGAS, thus, discouraging them from using their collections for personal or other purposes.
We further recommended that the Liquidating Officer must monitor the remittance of Barangays before an AF be released to the latter by requiring presentation of collection reports duly received by the Office of the Municipal Accountant and copy of validated deposit slips to ensure that collections were accordingly deposited/remitted.

We reiterated our recommendation to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare and submit the required monthly BRS pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No.96-011 dated October 2, 1996.  We further recommended that the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer prioritize reconciliation between cashbooks and accounting records as against the bank statements considering the huge amount of variances thereof. Any errors and reconciling items discovered in the reconciliation process must accordingly be adjusted in the respective records which needs correction/revision in order to come up with a more reliable/accurate balances of the Cash in Bank accounts as reflected in the accounting, cashbooks and bank records.
In addition, require the Office of the Municipal Accountant to strictly maintain SLs of the Cash in Bank account transactions for all funds per bank account of the Agency with AGDBs. Preferably record monthly transaction in the SL per specific Bank Account number in order to facilitate BRS preparation.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an Adjusting Journal Entry thru Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) in order to reflect the correct value of the Fixed Asset accounts by capitalizing the interest and related bank charges on the loan availed with DBP for the Acquisition of one (1) set garbage granulator with brick-making machine and Construction of Solid Waste Management Facility. Below is the suggested journal entry:

Account Description | Debit | Credit
Other Machinery & Equipment/Solid Waste Management Facility (Appropriate Asset Accounts-Pro rated) | ₱1,514,738.99 | 
Prior Years’ Adjustment |  | ₱1,514,738.99

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Officials/employees concerned to:
Submit a copy the approved Loan Agreement entered into by and between the Municipal Government and DBP, amendments/changes of the Loan Agreement, if any, and other supporting documents relevant to the grant of loan; and
Submit copies of the MOAs on fund transfers from NGAs for CY 2021 and comply the submission thereon to the Audit Team for the ensuing funds that will be downloaded from NGAs to the LGU within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 for a timely conduct of its auditorial review.

We recommended that Management require the BAC, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Engineer to immediately submit to the Audit Team the disbursement accounts and copies of contracts with its supporting/related documents relative to the above-mentioned PPAs for CY 2021 20% Development Fund.
We further recommended to strictly observe the prescribed timelines in the submission of awarded contracts and accounts under the 20% Development Fund in the ensuing year and onwards.

We recommended that the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 be complied with by subjecting the assessment process of project identification/ designing programs to be included in the GPB through the administration of the GeRL Assessment Tool. This is recommended in order to validate the identification of PPAs in the GPB of the Agency which are based on actual gender issues/related current concerns in the locality and ensure that the LGU have addressed a gender responsive local governance as one of the basic services.
We further recommended the submission of the duly accomplished GeRL Assessment Tool to the Audit Team for verification of GAD assessment of the LGU as part of the required GAD reports.

We recommended that Management direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to closely coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to determine the amount of unexpended balances of the LDRRMF and prepare a Revised LDRRMFIP for CY 2022 incorporating the PPAs to be funded from the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF balances.  Henceforth, the aforementioned provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 must be strictly implemented for the speedy realization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management objectives.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive must instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the LDRRMF and other reports using formats provided and as prescribed in the aforementioned circulars every 15th day after the end of every month. Henceforth, the aforementioned provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 on the reportorial requirements for the MDRRMF must be strictly followed on a monthly basis. Prompt submission of the mandated reports will facilitate timely review and verification of the Agency’s MDRRMF transactions, thus, corrective measures can be recommended, if necessary.

We recommended that the Management must revisit the LDRRMIP to strategize the implementation of its PPAs in order to have optimum utilization of the LDRRMF to attain the delivery of desired objectives on disaster risk management of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management must reassess the manpower at the Office of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management by prioritizing the creation of its required plantilla positions and augment the needed workforce in accordance with the provisions set forth in R.A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01 in order to reinforce personnel that will handle the expected outputs and deliverables, particularly on the reportorial, preparation and execution of plans relative to disaster risk management of the Municipality.
We further recommended appropriate trainings be extended to MDRRMO personnel to effectively perform their tasks which is significant towards the attainment in building a disaster-resilient local communities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to institute appropriate control measures to meet the required timelines on the submission of documents and reports to the Office of the Auditor. Identify the specific phase of financial transactions causing the delay or affecting its timely submission/non-submission, which is a perennial problem of the LGU, and address the constraints to comply with the prompt submission. Further, maximize the existing manpower or augment the required workforce, if necessary, to strengthen the Office of the Municipal Accountant in order to accomplish the required deliverables and meet the legal deadlines on reports/accounts submission.
Further, require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the paid vouchers/payrolls and issued ORs on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for recording in the agency books of accounts, after which, the same must be submitted to the Auditor within the first 10 days of the ensuing month including their supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that Offices of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant closely coordinate in the regular reconciliation of the ledgers as against the cash books and bank statements affecting the Cash in Bank accounts. We suggested that the entries in the cash books of the Accountable Officers at the Office of the Municipal Treasurer be likewise considered in the reconciliation process to come up with a more reliable and accurate balances of the Cash in Bank accounts as reflected in the cash books and accounting records.

We reiterated our recommendation to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the required monthly BRS pursuant to Section 74 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No.96-011 dated October 2, 1996.  Moreover, the data reflected in the cash books of the Accountable Officers must preferably be considered in the preparation of the BRS to ensure that any reconciling items will be accordingly adjusted.

In addition, require the Office of the Municipal Accountant to revisit the maintenance/appropriate entries in the Subsidiary Ledgers of the Cash in Bank account transactions for all funds to preferably record per specific Bank Account number to facilitate BRS preparation. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately undertake the reconciliation process and any errors/reconciling items discovered thereof must accordingly be adjusted in the respective records which needs correction/revision to arrive at a correct and reliable cash in bank balance.
observations.managementAction: Partial reconciliation between the Accounting and Treasury records has been undertaken.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower at the Office of the Municipal Accountant. Further discussed in Part 2 of this report under Audit Observation No. 2.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require all employees/Accountable Officers to settle their respective cash advances at the end of the year to reflect the correct balances of the accounts affected in the books of accounts of the Agency.  Henceforth, avoid granting subsequent cash advances to officials/Accountable Officers with outstanding cash advances and withhold the salaries and/or any amount due to them who were unable to comply with the pertinent rules and regulations governing cash advances.  Adhere strictly to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and settlement of cash advances as enunciated in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Cause for the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances amounting to ₱2.69 million as of December 31, 2020 and submit the liquidation report or proof of refund/s, if there is any, to the Audit Team for post-audit. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to send Demand Letters to the concerned Accountable Officer(s), agency officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant limits the granting of some cash advances such as those who still have unliquidated cash advances. The bulk of current year cash advances were liquidated as of December 31, 2021.
observations.reason: Several officials and employees were still unable to settle their unliquidated cash advances at the end of the year. (Please see related finding under Part II-Audit Observation No. 3).

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant prompt preparation of the disbursement vouchers with the required supporting documents covering the remittances of the collections received, prepare the checks for the said remittances and transmit to the concerned agencies on or before the due date to avoid surcharges and penalties. Further, see to it that officials concerned be conscientious in the observance of the deadlines set by the respective government offices concerned. In addition, the latter must install strict monitoring of personnel assigned thereof for the prompt remittance and avoid piling up of monthly balances of Trust Liabilities in order to ensure continuous benefits of employees offered by the concerned government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all insurable properties be insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS in the ensuing year and onwards as well as see to it that monitoring measures are in place in the renewal before its due date. Ensure that funds are allocated to cover the premiums for the insurance so that the Municipality will be indemnified or compensated for any damage or losses arising from the risks the properties were insured for.

observations.reason: The Agency has yet to identify priority LGU assets and its serviceability to be insured considering limited funds allocated for insurance premiums.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other Municipal Officials concerned shall ensure the completeness of supporting documents before approving any disbursement voucher for payment and strictly observe the timelines in the submission of accounts in order to ascertain the legality and validity of all claims against the Bayanihan Grant fund received by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Submitted documents is still for evaluation yet.
observations.reason: The concerned officials were not strictly observing the required timelines in the submission of documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management through the MSWDO and in coordination with the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer the following measures to rectify the deficiencies noted:

Submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents;

Ensure that the necessary documentary requirements are complied with before release/distribution of SAP funds is made to avoid audit sanctions; and

Strictly observe the timelines in the submission of accounts/reports to the Audit Team. Meantime, it is suggested that the Municipal Accountant maximize the existing workforce at the Accounting Section and introduce measures to meet the required timelines.
observations.managementAction: Some documents were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Due to limited manpower to handle the downloaded financial transactions considering the volume of SAP transactions involved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Office of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) thru the BAC Chairman to furnish the Audit Team within five working days all perfected contracts/approved POs and the supporting documents from the date of its execution.

In addition, the Office of the BAC must revisit the flow of documentation process to see to it that the LGU could meet the required timelines in the submission and to assign specific personnel to take charge of its compliance.
observations.managementAction: Some contracts under the 20 per cent Development Fund for CY 2021 were submitted to the Audit Team but way beyond the required timelines.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat was not able to observe the required timelines on the submission of required documents to the Audit Team. (Please see related finding per Audit Observation No. 7).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the required documentations on all of the implemented projects/programs under the 20 per cent Development Fund and Trust Fund accounts. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Audit Team the disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱18.35 million and ₱3.72 million or an aggregate total of ₱22.07 million under the 20 per cent Development Fund and Trust Fund accounts. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to refrain from issuing checks and the corresponding Accountant’s Advice unless complete documentations are accordingly complied.

Ensure strict adherence, in the ensuing year and onwards, to Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 governing the prompt submission of accounts which include the expenditures on PPAs undertaken by the LGU for CY 2020, to give ample time to the Audit Team for evaluating the economy, efficiency and effectiveness aspects of the implemented PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to submit Disbursement and Collection accounts up to July 2021 transactions.
observations.reason: Laxity of the concerned LGU Officials in complying the required documentations and monitoring thereof. Further discussed in Part 2 of this report under Audit Observation No. 7.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:

The Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain CIPLC for each on-going infrastructure project. Trace out the records pertaining to the prior year’s CIP accounts and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in securing the copies of accomplishment report (AR) on the implemented projects and on-going projects on previous and current years. Likewise, both Offices must undertake periodic reconciliation of records on CIP accounts and consider the conduct of inventory as of every year end;

The Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer whose powers and duties requires him to take custody of and be accountable for all properties, real or personal, owned by the Agency, to secure the copy of the Monthly Status Report of Projects Undertaken from the Engineering Office and validate the existence of the ongoing infrastructure projects to attest to the correctness of the accounting records; and

The Municipal Accountant to maintain the RPI to record the cost of completed public infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Year-end Financial Statements have accompanying schedules for the Construction-in-Progress account. However, some have no details rather on generic description.
observations.reason: Limited manpower at the Office of the Municipal Accountant and workload assigned to the Municipal Treasurer with concurrent capacity as Property Officer contributed to non-compliance of the audit recommendations.

observations.managementAction: Liquidations of downloaded funds are usually submitted to the Audit Team but only when there are succeeding funds to be released but pending due to previously unliquidated funds released.
observations.reason: We noted that no focal person monitored its prompt liquidations, including refunds of excess funds from completed projects, to donor agencies. Moreover, lack of manpower at the Office of the Municipal Engineer wherein only 1 plantilla position is assigned thereat.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit a written explanation for the non-implementation of various PPAs in the year 2019. Henceforth, ensure that the 20 per cent of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development.  Both Executive and Legislative Offices must be conscientious with their duties and functions particularly in the deliberation and approval of the utilization of the fund within the prescribed period in order to accomplish and achieve its objective.

Strict observance of timetable for the implementation of specific phase of activities to be undertaken will aid the Agency in prioritizing activities to be implemented given the available resources for that period to achieve results.

Implement immediately the programs and projects relative to the reprogrammed/ unutilized fund amounting to ₱23.46 million.
observations.managementAction: The Agency was able to implement and completed 15 PPAs under the 20% DF for CY 2021.
observations.reason: Due to pandemic and weather conditions. Also, PRRDP funds were not yet downloaded to the LGU despite the availability of LGU counterpart which form part of the unimplemented PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Designated MDRRM Officer in close coordination with the MDRRMC to prepare the annual LDRRMFIP to coincide with the budget preparation of the LGU, as basis for the LDRRMF budget to be incorporated in the Annual Budget of the LGU. Likewise, in the LDRRMFIP, include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years which must be implemented as planned in order to optimize utilization and bring the desired benefits to its constituents and to avoid future reversion of the fund to the General Fund.  Strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 is required.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMFIP for CY 2021 has been prepared & submitted. However, unexpended LDRRMF of previous years were not included in the current year.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and due to inadvertence since the LGU interpreted that prior years unexpended balances will not be included in the annual plan since it was already included in the 5-year LDRRMFIP. Further discussed in Part 2 of this report under Audit Observation No. 9.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM.  Ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF and LDRRM-MOOE for each year be timely transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back up in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash and liability accounts in the financial statements; and

The Accounting Office must coordinate with the Budget Office to reconcile their records and obtain the correct amount of the unexpended balances QRF, LDRRM-MOOE and LDRRMF-CO for the current year and previous years.
observations.managementAction: Prior years LDRRMF unutilized balances were transferred to the Trust Fund account.
observations.reason: Lack of strategy and extra efforts to comply within the time frame to transfer the      LDRRMF balance for the current year.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Agency strictly comply with the procedure and accounting treatment in the recording of Real Property Tax and Special Education tax as prescribed under Section 248 of R.A. 7160 and Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS for Local Government Units, Volume I.

Likewise, Management must require the Municipal Assessor to submit the required RPTAR based on the approved Revised Assessments and Property Classification for CY 2013.  Moreover, see to it that copies of the revised assessment were issued to taxpayers before demanding the exact payment of taxes due them.  Strict adherence to Sections 219 and 223 is required.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the lump-sum amount of various remittances from barangays and identify specific names of barangays with the corresponding amount due. After which, require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately release the respective amount payable to the 35 barangays of the Municipal Government as well as to the Province of Masbate, as mandated under the existing regulations.

Further, to require the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the guidelines relative to the prompt release of the barangay shares from realty taxes as stated under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code and shares from CTCs collections in the ensuing years.
observations.managementAction: The LCE included this concern to be taken up during LIGA meeting.
observations.reason: Some Barangays have no Accountable Form 51 to acknowledge receipt of Barangay share from the Municipal accounts due to some constraints on their unremitted collection accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require both the Accounting and Budget Offices to strictly comply with the requirement in the preparation of SCBAA pursuant to IPSAS 24.  See to it that the budgetary basis and classification basis adopted in the approved budget are included in the Notes to Financial Statements. It is understood that the SCBAA is prepared on a comparable basis, otherwise a Separate SCBAA is presented to ensure that: a) readers do not misinterpret financial information that is prepared on different bases; b) the financial statements could usefully clarify that the budget and the accounting bases differ; and c)that the SCBAA is prepared on the budget basis. (paragraph 23 of IPSAS 24).

We recommended that Management:

Require the Local Finance Committee to conduct thorough evaluation of the budget proposals pertaining to the F/P/A under Lump sum Appropriations; consider the analysis of appropriation and expenditure items in conformity with the Standard Government Chart of Accounts; and require the WFP before the issuance of AA in order to determine and monitor the accomplishments as against the targets;

Require the necessary authorization by the Sangguniang Bayan for the LCE to enter into: a) contract for projects described in generic terms; and b) contract for purchase of goods and services for lump sum appropriation; and

Require the submission of MOA for fund assistance to other National Government Agencies, approved Program of Work and approved project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses indicating the project/program objective and expected outputs. For recording purposes of the fund assistance, proper coordination with the recipient agency must be undertaken.
observations.reason: Copies of MOA for downloaded funds from NGAs were not submitted to the Audit Team due to lack of designated focal person/s to monitor its compliance and see to it the LGU be furnished with a perfected copy of MOA or its equivalent document. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the required documentations on all of the implemented projects/programs under the 20 per cent Development Fund and Trust Fund accounts. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit to the Audit Team the disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱18.35 million and ₱3.72 million or an aggregate total of ₱22.07 million under the 20 per cent Development Fund and Trust Fund accounts. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to refrain from issuing checks and the corresponding Accountant’s Advice unless complete documentations are accordingly complied.
Ensure strict adherence, in the ensuing year and onwards, to Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 governing the prompt submission of accounts which include the expenditures on PPAs undertaken by the LGU for CY 2020, to give ample time to the Audit Team for evaluating the economy, efficiency and effectiveness aspects of the implemented PPAs.

We recommended that the provisions of Section 3 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 be complied with by subjecting the assessment process of project identification and designing programs to be prioritized in the inclusion of PPAs for GAD, through the administration of the HGDG Checklist/Tool in order to ensure that the same favorably addressed/responsive to the gender issues and related current concerns in the locality.

We recommended that Management, through the Local Finance Committee and in coordination with the Local School Board, ensure that the total share in the additional one per cent tax on real property from the previous year be fully appropriated in the succeeding year’s SEF Annual Budget. Compliance thereof will ensure optimum funding of priority programs, projects and activities of public schools in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive reinforce the manpower at the Office of the Municipal Accountant to cope up with the required deliverables of the Agency, specifically on the observance of timelines in the submission of accounts/reports to the Audit Team. Meantime, it is suggested that the Municipal Accountant maximize the existing workforce at the Accounting Section and introduce measures to meet the required timelines. In addition, instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly refrain from allowing payment of claims without complete documentations and notify all Municipal Officials/Employees on such directive for strict compliance.
Further, require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to regularly and promptly forward the paid vouchers/payrolls accounts and issued AFs to the Office of the Municipal Accountant on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month, to ensure compliance with the required deadlines for appropriate recording in the Agency’s books of accounts. After which, the same be submitted to the Auditor within the first 10 days of the ensuing month including their supporting documents pursuant to Section 107 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006.

Require the Office of the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit Accountant’s Advices of Local Check Disbursements for all funds of the LGU. See to it that copies of Accountant’s Advice in the ensuing period be regularly and completely submitted to the Audit Team as mandated in COA Circular No. 96-007 dated May 15, 1996. We suggested that the Office of the Municipal Accountant closely coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Mayor to ensure that all checks signed by the Local Chief Executive would be incorporated in the corresponding Accountant’s Advice which must be forwarded to the Auditor on a weekly basis for all funds per bank account check issuances.
Strict compliance on the recommendation offered would manifest strengthening the internal control system on the disbursements process of the Agency. Prompt submission of Accountant’s Advices would facilitate timely review and verification of the Agency’s financial transactions wherein corrective measures can be implemented, if necessary.

We recommended that Management require all Accountable Officers to settle their respective cash advances at the end of the year to reflect the correct balances of the accounts affected in the books of accounts of the Agency.  Henceforth, avoid granting subsequent cash advances to Accountable Officers with outstanding cash advances and withhold salaries and/or any amount due to those Accountable Officers who were unable to comply with pertinent rules and regulations on cash advances. Adhere strictly to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and settlement of cash advances as enunciated in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Cause for the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash advances amounting to ₱2.97 million as of December 31, 2019 and submit the liquidation report to the Auditor for post-audit. Send Demand Letters to the concerned Accountable Officer(s) with unliquidated cash advances for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant limits the granting of some cash advances such as those who still have unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Several officials and employees were still unable to settle their unliquidated cash advances at the end of the year. (Please see related finding under Audit Observation No. 2).

observations.recommendation: Considering that the Municipality has the available cash in bank balance, we recommended requiring the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit or deposit the withheld collections due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.  Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to prepare the schedule of monthly remittances and disbursement vouchers (DVs) and immediately prepare and release the checks to them.  Prompt remittances of these trust liabilities must be done to avoid possible misuse of cash resources and incurrence of unnecessary penalties that may be imposed by the concerned agencies.  Further, it is reminded that payments covering the said penalties and surcharges on the late remittances must not be charged to the government funds but instead a personal liability of the concerned officials who were unable to comply with the mandated laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Agency was able to significantly decrease the total trust collections in CY 2020 as compared to CY 2019 balances by ₱1.68 million.
observations.reason: Please see related finding under Audit Observation No. 4.

observations.managementAction: Liquidation Reports for some downloaded funds were forwarded to the Audit Team in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Please see related finding under Audit Observation No. 10.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit a written explanation for the non-implementation of various PPAs in the year 2019. Henceforth, ensure that the 20 per cent of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development.  Both Executive and Legislative Offices must be conscientious with their duties and functions particularly in the deliberation and approval of the utilization of the fund within the prescribed period in order to accomplish and achieve its objective.

Strict observance of timetable for the implementation of specific phase of activities to be undertaken will aid the Agency in prioritizing activities to be implemented given the available resources for that period to achieve results.

Implement immediately the programs and projects relative to the reprogrammed/ unutilized fund amounting to ₱23.46 million.
observations.managementAction: The Management was aware of the low utilization of 20% EDF and assured to exert best efforts to provide more manpower to the Office of the Municipal Engineer in order to closely monitor the implementation of programs/ projects within the desired timelines.
observations.reason: Low utilization of 20% EDF was due to lack of manpower especially technical personnel in-charge in the preparation of the detailed engineering designs, program of works and other relevant engineering documents. Evaluation of records showed that the 20%  Development Fund allocation for CY 2020 amounting to ₱34.21 million has an unutilized balance of ₱22.92 million or 66.98% as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Designated MDRRM Officer in close coordination with the MDRRMC to prepare the annual LDRRMFIP to coincide with the budget preparation of the LGU, as basis for the LDRRMF budget to be incorporated in the Annual Budget of the LGU. Likewise, in the LDRRMFIP, include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years which must be implemented as planned in order to optimize utilization and bring the desired benefits to its constituents and to avoid future reversion of the fund to the General Fund.  Strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 is required.
observations.managementAction: The Management made the necessary actions to help the MDRRMC to come up with a comprehensive and well-planned /studied LDRRMFIP.
observations.reason: Inability of the MDRRMC to prepare a comprehensive and well-planned/studied LDRRMFIP and submit the same to the Audit Team for audit reference.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM.  Ensure that the unexpended balance of QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE for each year be timely transferred at year-end with the corresponding cash back up in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash and liability accounts in the financial statements; and

The Accounting Office must coordinate with the Budget Office to reconcile their records and obtain the correct amount of the unexpended balances QRF, LDRRM-MOOE and LDRRMF-CO for the current year and previous years.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance as of Dec. 31, 2019 amounting to ₱2.46 million was properly transferred to the Trust Fund account as of Dec. 31, 2020. However, the corresponding LDRRMF unexpended balance for CY 2020 was not accordingly transferred to the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Due to time constraints and manpower, the Agency was unable to promptly transfer the LDRRMF unexpended balance at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately all accounts and financial reports under each fund covering the period October to December 2019 as well as the lacking accounts for the months of May to September 2019.  Proper division/distribution of accounting workload be made and proper delegation to other employees be done. Also, Management must consider timely submission of reports as among the primary measures of good governance and performance for the audit to review on time the transactions and operations of the Municipality.

Moreover, we recommended that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer submit the monthly accounts on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month to the Office of the Municipal Accountant to ensure compliance with the required deadlines.  See to it that complete documentation has been attached in the DVs and approved by authorized officials.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to submit Disbursement and Collection accounts up to August 2020 transactions. CY 2020 year-end reports were submitted on March 23, 2021.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower. Further discussed in Part 2 of this report under Audit Observation No. 13.

observations.recommendation:

observations.reason: The GAD Focal Person was unable to submit the GAD data base which will serve as gender statistics and GAD-related information crucial for evidence-based planning and policy-making. (See related finding under Observation No. 11).

observations.recommendation:

observations.reason: Lack of coordination between the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant & Market Supervisor. Hence, receivables from Uson Public Market were not accordingly recorded in the books of accounts of the LGU as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require both the Accounting and Budget Offices to strictly comply with the requirement in the preparation of SCBAA pursuant to IPSAS 24. It is understood that the SCBAA is prepared on a comparable basis, otherwise a Separate SCBAA is presented to ensure that: a) readers do not misinterpret financial information that is prepared on different bases; b) the financial statements could usefully clarify that the budget and the accounting bases differ; and c)that the SCBAA is prepared on the budget basis. (paragraph 23 of IPSAS 24).
observations.managementAction: The Offices of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant are  exerting extra efforts to improve and in order that SCBAA could be relied upon. However, periodic reconciliation of their respective records is suggested.

The Accounting Office must coordinate with the Budget Office to reconcile their records and obtain the correct amount of the unexpended balances QRF, LDRRM-MOOE and LDRRMF-CO for the current year and previous years.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO, in close coordination with the MDRRMC, to prepare a well-planned/studied LDRRMFIP which must also include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years which must be implemented as planned in order to optimize utilization and bring the desired benefits to its constituents and to avoid future reversion of the fund to the General fund. observations.managementAction: The management made the necessary actions to help the MDRRMC to come up with well-planned /studied LDRRMFIP. The said unexpended balance of ₱3.8 million was properly transferred to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require both the Accounting and Budget Offices to strictly comply with the requirement in the preparation of SCBAA pursuant to IPSAS 24. (paragraph 23 of IPSAS 24).
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant are doing the necessary action to improve and in order that Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts could be relied upon, quarterly reconciliation of records will be undertaken.
observations.reason: Lack of reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation:

Require the necessary authorization by the Sangguniang Bayan for the LCE to enter into: a) contract for projects described in generic terms; and b) contract for purchase of goods and services for lump sum appropriation; and
Require the submission of MOA for fund assistance to other National Government Agencies, approved Program of Work and approved project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses indicating the project/program objective and expected outputs. observations.reason: Because several concerned offices did not submit their work and financial plan to our Municipal Budget Officer.

observations.recommendation:

Henceforth, ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development. Both Executive and Legislative Offices must be conscientious with their duties and functions particularly in the deliberation and approval of the utilization of the fund within the prescribed period in order to accomplish and achieve its objective.

Strict observance of timetable for the implementation of specific phase of activities to be undertaken will aid the agency in prioritizing activities to be implemented given the available resources for that period to achieve results.

Implement immediately the programs and projects relative to the unutilized funds of Ᵽ25,791,160.80 as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, out of the total appropriation of ₱27,825,000.00 under the 20% Development Fund, the municipality has obligated ₱10,069,045.32 or 36.19% utilization only.
observations.reason: Poor monitoring of targeted plans as against actual implementation of PPAs as projected due to lack of manpower in the Office of the Municipal Engineer.

observations.managementAction: The LCE has just appointed a full time MDRRMO who will hopefully enhance the needed output in LDRRMF related concerns of the municipality.
observations.reason: Due to poor monitoring of DRRM funds and targets, the LGU has only utilized P651,164.00 or 8.64% out of the total MDRRMF appropriations amounting to P7,535,651.44 for CY 2018. Moreover, prior years MDRRMF balances amounting to P17.4 million were unutilized as well as of Dec. 31, 2018.  Please see related Audit Observation  No. 10.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to transfer all unutilized balances of the LDRRMF to the TF bank account and record the transferrin the TF books. See to it that the fund will be incorporated in the LDRMMFIP. Likewise, require the DRRMO to submit Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund in accordance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002
observations.managementAction: The CY 2017 LDRRMF unexpended balance was already transferred to Trust Fund in the ensuing year 2018. However, no compliance for CY 2018 unexpended balance was undertaken.
observations.reason: Please see related Audit Observation  No. 9.

observations.recommendation:
The GAD Focal Point System must establish a monitoring and evaluation system to periodically assess the implementation of the projects and programs as planned. The Management must require the officials concerned to fully implement the GAD activities.

Further, ensure that the DILG, Regional Office issue the Certificate of Endorsement and effect its comment/s and recommendation/s on the submitted LGU Plan and Budget (GPB) and GAD Accomplishment Report (GAD AR) to see to it that it is gender responsive and have addressed the priority needs relative to the prevailing GAD concerns in accordance with the “Guide in the Review and Endorsement of LGU GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and Review of GAD Accomplishment Report (GAD AR)” as required under JMC No. 2016-01 by PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, we have noted that out of the allocated amount ₱7,535,651.45 for the implementation of the CY 2018 GAD related programs, projects and activities, the agency was able to utilized  ₱4,056,650.15 or 53.83% of which were and ₱3,479,001.30 or 46.17% were unexpended at year end.
observations.reason: The GAD Focal Point System of the municipality is not strengthened which resulted in poor monitoring and evaluation of the GAD PPAs if it had addressed the identified gender issues/concerns in the locality.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the requirements with respect to the prompt submission of the monthly reports, trial balance and other financial reports to acquaint the chief executive of what happened in the agency and for early disclosure of accountability to improve performance efficiency. Prompt submission of these reports are required for early detection of errors for a sound financial position of the municipality.

Institute appropriate disciplinary actions against those officials/employees who were responsible for the delayed preparation and/or non-submission of the required financial reports within the prescribed period, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The CYs 2017 & 2018 year-end FSs were submitted on March 23, 2018 & April 26, 2019, respectively. Nevertheless, the Office of the Municipal Accountant has exerted best efforts to partially comply with the submission timelines. See restated finding-Audit Observation No. 7.
observations.reason:
Lack of manpower at the Office of the Mun. Accountant.
The agency was not able to submit the CYs 2017 & 2018 monthly accounts/reports within the required deadlines.  See restated finding-Audit Observation No. 7 for additional details.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Solid Waste Management Board, the Municipal Planning Office and all the Departments involved in planning and conceptualizing the Solid Waste Management Plan to give their full attention and cooperation in the revision and finalization of the draft Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU to be able to comply with the requirement of the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

Appoint a permanent employee who will be in-charge of the operation of the solid waste management board.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, the MPDC informed the Audit Team that that the SWM Plan was already revised but still under review as of this writing.
observations.reason: Poor coordination and linkages with LGU officials and concerned OGAs who were in charge of the review  and approval of the plans emanating from the LGU level.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately and in full all undeposited collections with the authorized depository bank. Furthermore, require the collectors to immediately remit in full the undeposited collections as of year-end. Moving forward, remit their collections daily to avoid incurrence of shortages and/or possible misuse of public funds and refrain from releasing additional accountable forms unless previous ones were fully accounted for. Strict adherence to the abovementioned rules and regulations relative to the collections and deposits is required.
observations.managementAction: Collections balance was substantially reduced as of December 31, 2018. Previous years balances were deposited at year-end.
observations.reason: We have noted that the collections were not still deposited in full and intact as mandated in the existing guidelines due to the laxity of the Liquidating Officer in the observation of collections and deposits related timelines.

observations.recommendation:
Enforce all accountable officers to settle all their respective cash advances at the end of the year to reflect the correct balances of the accounts affected in the financial statements. Henceforth, avoid granting subsequent cash advances and withhold salaries and/or any amount due those accountable officers who did not comply with pertinent rules and regulations on cash advances. Adhere strictly to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and settlement of cash advances as enunciated in COA Circular No. 98-002 dated February 10, 1997.

Direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit all liquidation reports and the corresponding supporting documents on the recorded settlement of cash advances granted to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The agency had partially liquidated previous years cash advance balances. However, the Audit Team still noted some deficiencies relative thereto. See restated finding-Audit Observation No. 1.
observations.reason: Unliquidated cash advances amounted to Ᵽ2.53 million as of December 31, 2018. Additional details-please see related Audit Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation: Immediately, an inventory committee be created to conduct the physical inventory of all supplies or property of the agency composed of three (3) consisting of the (1) representative of the local chief executive as chairman; (2) the general services officer, municipal or barangay treasurer, as the case may be; and (3) the supply accountable officer of the department or office concerned as members. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain records on all properties (land and buildings) account and coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Engineer for the complete details. See to it that the submitted inventory report is reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction:
The Office of the Municipal Treasurer was only able to submit a mere listing of LGU’s properties as of December 31, 2018.
(Restated finding under Audit Observation No. 3).
observations.reason:
The agency has not established a reliable Inventory Committee to conduct the required inventory and its reporting phase. 
(Please see related finding-Audit Observation No. 3)

observations.recommendation:
Establish immediately the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 through the execution of appropriate issuances which must be furnished to concerned officials for information dissemination.

Further, strengthen the duly constituted GFPS thru capability building and trainings so that the members may be able to perform its general and specific functions as enumerated and discussed in detail under the same Joint Memorandum Circular and therefore the planning and formulation taking into account the priority gender and empowerment issues that needs to be addressed, implementation of GAD activities and monitoring the same be effectively and efficiently undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly observe Section 13 of IRR of RA No. 9184. Revisit the provisions regarding the appropriate Observers on specific type of programs/projects/activities to be bid out to enhance the procurement process of the agency. Ensure the BAC Secretariat is well acquainted with the necessary documentary requirements for the auditorial and technical review of all contracts entered into by the Local Government Unit on its programs/projects/activities implemented.

Direct the officials and employees concerned to strictly comply with the prescribed laws, rules and regulations on the Documentary Requirements for the Evaluation of the Technical Aspect of all Government Contracts and Inspection of Government Projects per COA Memoranda Nos. 91-704 dated February 27, 1991 and 2005-027 dated February 28, 2005. Remind the Office of the Municipal Accountant to require the attachment of complete supporting documents on the duly processed disbursement vouchers pertaining to procurement transactions of the municipality, as prescribed in Section 9.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Prepare a monthly monitoring report of the Project Status for verification by the COA Technical Audit Specialist.
observations.managementAction: The agency has partially submitted the required documentations to the Audit Team which is still subject to review and evaluation.

observations.recommendation: Adhere to the provisions of Section 53 (e) of the IRR-A, RA No. 9184 and Administrative Order No.17 dated July 28, 2011 relative to the procurement of common-use supplies from the Procurement Service Office, DBM. Comply with DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 A and submit the Annual Procurement Plan for common Use Supplies and Equipment for consolidation by the PS.

observations.reason: Feedback from the agency disclosed that some supplies were not available at the the Procurement Service Office-DBM and if available were of poor quality and no stable status on its availability in a certain date set.

observations.recommendation: Strictly adhere to Sections 7, 8 and 9 of COA Circular No.97-001 and Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 governing the rendition of accounts and submission of copies of contracts. Direct the BAC Secretariat, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to closely coordinate in the compliance of the required documentation and submission to the Audit Team. Require the Office of the Municipal Accountant to require the attachment of complete supporting documents on the duly processed disbursement vouchers pertaining to infrastructure projects before effecting the payment.
